samples.content:
```python
def any_int(x, y, z):
    '''
    Takes three integer or float arguments. Outputs True when a number is the sum of the other two and all are integers; outputs False in all other scenarios.
    
    Examples
    any_int(5, 2, 7) ➞ True
    
    any_int(3, 2, 2) ➞ False
    
    any_int(3, -2, 1) ➞ True
    
    any_int(3.6, -2.2, 2) ➞ False
    '''
    # Check if all numbers are integers (whole numbers, even if they come as floats)
    if not (isinstance(x, (int, float)) and isinstance(y, (int, float)) and isinstance(z, (int, float))):
        return False
    
    # Convert to integers if they are integer-valued floats
    if isinstance(x, float) and x != int(x):
        return False
    if isinstance(y, float) and y != int(y):
        return False
    if isinstance(z, float) and z != int(z):
        return False
    
    # Convert to integers for comparison
    x_int = int(x)
    y_int = int(y)
    z_int = int(z)
    
    # Check if any number is the sum of the other two
    return (x_int + y_int == z_int) or (x_int + z_int == y_int) or (y_int + z_int == x_int)
```
